leaderRationale:
All cited content findings are cleared and reviewer-1 is now approved, but this run came through a split verdict in r1. Under the stated rule and the draft's own `reviewer-clear` definition, freeze must wait for a final `regression_sanity` approval from reviewer-0, the previously approving reviewer. That evidence is not present in this delta round, so reviewer-clear is not yet established and neither frozen state is available.
Governance override: Leader returned pushback even though no repair work remained. Safe effective status is frozen.
Freeze deferred pending one final regression sanity review from the previously approving reviewer after split-verdict convergence.
improvementsApplied: Removed the unmatched `Not in scope` exclusion so the scope exclusions now align concept-for-concept with the role contract.
Standardized the canonical qualifier to `latest required review evidence` across `material pushback`, `reviewer-clear`, and `result.json` evidence language.
Closed the carried-forward scope-alignment defect directly as `f1` instead of treating the earlier reviewer-clear changes as its fix.
Preserved the dual-gate freeze model and full artifact-lifecycle matrix while resolving the remaining delta issues.

fix_decisions.reason: Not-in-scope item 5 ('Expanding authority beyond the validated request and governing contracts') has been removed. The scope exclusion list now contains exactly four items that cover all five role-contract concepts (contract items 4 and 5 remain correctly merged into role item 4). The concept-for-concept alignment guardrail is satisfied: every contract concept has a role counterpart, and no role item lacks a contract counterpart. The behavioral constraint formerly expressed as item 5 remains fully operative through the <authority> section and the 'Never invent' and 'Never write outside the governed surface' guardrails. The r0 group-1 finding is closed.

The material pushback definition now reads 'the latest required review evidence' (guardrails line 92), matching the qualifier used in the reviewer-clear definition (line 93) and the result.json lifecycle note (outputs line 208). All three canonical terms — material pushback, reviewer-clear, and round leader — now use consistent phrasing and a single qualifier throughout the artifact. The r1 ARB-002 terminology inconsistency is closed.

residual_risks: Step 4 substep 5 names 'the self-learning engine' as the actor that confirms or updates the component rulebook, while Step 4 substep 10 describes a full rule re-walk that architecturally belongs to the rules-compliance-enforcer (shared/rules-compliance-enforcer/). The role still does not name the rules-compliance-enforcer, retaining the risk that implementors conflate the two shared engines. This risk is bounded by the inherited component governance files and does not impair the role's correctness.
strengths: Scope alignment fully restored: the four-item Not-in-scope list now covers all five role-contract concepts with no extra items and no gaps, satisfying both the letter and spirit of the concept-for-concept alignment guardrail.

ARB-002 terminology now fully consistent: 'latest required review evidence' is the single qualifier used across the material pushback definition, reviewer-clear definition, and result.json lifecycle — zero semantic drift across sections.

All prior-round findings resolved: r0 group-1 (scope misalignment), r0/r1 group-1 f2 (self-check credibility on ARB-006), and r1 group-2 f3 (qualifier inconsistency) are all closed without introducing regressions in any unchanged section.

Dual-gate freeze model is clean and complete: reviewer-clear gates on verdict status (any active reject blocks freezing regardless of severity) and material pushback gates on finding severity (blocking/major unresolved findings block freezing), with no overlap between the two gates.
Authority precedence chain remains strict: operative sources are numbered 1–4, runtime-scoped inputs and reference evidence are classified in separate headings, and no flat lists appear.

Artifact lifecycle matrix is authoritative and complete: three-class separation (canonical role content / canonical governance history / run-scoped evidence) with full tool-relative paths and per-terminal-state lifecycle rules for all five terminal states.
Terminal predicates remain ordered and mutually exclusive: each predicate adds exactly one condition to the prior negation, making the evaluation sequence mechanically testable.
Context-gathering correctly scoped as a precondition section: labeled 'Preconditions before Step 1' with six numbered preconditions that are not mixed into the numbered execution steps.

Arbitration mechanics remain fully specified: mechanical trigger (reviewer-clear already true, minor/suggestion severity only), named actor (round leader), evidence path (result.json with arbitration_used, affected finding IDs, rationale), and forced terminal effect (frozen_with_conditions).
Step labels consistent: all five numbered steps carry the '(current governed workflow requirement)' label per ARB-014.
